home.subtitle: 3–15 friends. One phone. One bluffer.

home.howto.step_2: 2. Pass the phone around to secretly check your word.
home.howto.step_3: 3. One of you is the Imposter and only sees the word 'Imposter'.
home.howto.step_4: 4. Take turns saying ONE word to describe the secret word.

howToPlay.title: How to Play
howToPlay.intro.heading: The Game
howToPlay.intro.body: A pass-the-phone party game for 3 to 15 players. No internet needed — just one phone, your friends, and a knack for bluffing.
howToPlay.players.heading: Players & Imposters
howToPlay.players.body: Play with 3 to 15 friends. Choose 1 to 3 imposters depending on your group size. The bigger the crew, the more chaos.
howToPlay.deal.heading: 1. The Deal
howToPlay.deal.body: Pass the phone around. Each player privately reveals their secret word. Most players see the same word — but the imposters see only "IMPOSTER". Don't show your screen to anyone.
howToPlay.discuss.heading: 2. The Discussion
howToPlay.discuss.body: Take turns describing your word with hints — never say it directly. Imposters don't know the word, so they have to bluff and blend in by listening to the clues.
howToPlay.vote.heading: 3. The Vote
howToPlay.vote.body: When time runs out, everyone votes for who they think the imposter is. Pick wisely — one wrong guess and the imposter walks away with the win.
howToPlay.win.heading: Who Wins?
howToPlay.win.crew: Crew wins if they catch the imposter.
howToPlay.win.imposter: The imposter wins if they slip past the vote.
howToPlay.tips.heading: Pro Tips
howToPlay.tips.tip_1: Don't be too vague — vague clues look suspicious.
howToPlay.tips.tip_2: Don't be too specific — you'll give the word away.
howToPlay.tips.tip_3: Watch for hesitation, repeats, or generic answers.
howToPlay.tips.tip_4: If you're the imposter, react like you understand. Confidence sells the bluff.
howToPlay.tips.tip_5: Crewmates: vary your hints so the imposter can't just copy you.
